serving.natural_language_portion: One standard slice of honey cake as served at Toast Box Singapore, approximately 80 g.

nutrient_source.title: Cake, sponge, plain — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (NDB 18101); adapted with honey-cake composition reference from USDA Foundation Foods honey and butter enrichment
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: USDA SR Legacy sponge/plain cake (NDB 18101) used as the primary compositional anchor. Values adjusted upward for sugar and energy to reflect honey substitution (~15% honey by weight replacing part of sucrose), consistent with standard honey cake formulations. Saturated fat reflects butter-based recipe. This is an estimated adaptation; no Singapore-specific honey cake entry was found in HPB Foodo or FAO/INFOODS SEA at time of processing.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of sponge cake and honey cake analogues — University of Sydney GI Database; Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care 2008;31(12):2281-2283.
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement for Toast Box Honey Cake found. GI of 56 estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for sponge cake (GI ~46–66, mean ~56) and honey (GI ~58). Honey cake is a butter-enriched sponge with honey as a primary sweetener; the fat content moderates glycemic response relative to plain sponge. GL per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (56 × 45.6) / 100 ≈ 25.5, using 80 g serving × 57.0 g carbohydrate per 100 g = 45.6 g carbohydrate per serving.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content and refined carbohydrate load
hypertension.guidance: One slice of honey cake provides approximately 176 mg sodium (80 g serving); individuals with hypertension should limit intake to occasional consumption and monitor cumulative sodium from other meal components.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High sugar content and medium-to-high glycemic load
type2_diabetes.guidance: With approximately 25.6 g of sugar and a glycemic load of ~25.5 per serving, honey cake poses a significant postprandial glucose challenge for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes and should be consumed only occasionally in small portions with careful blood glucose monitoring.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat and cholesterol from butter and eggs
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Each serving contributes approximately 5.2 g saturated fat and 76 mg cholesterol; individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit honey cake to occasional treats and account for its saturated fat contribution within daily limits of less than 7% of total energy from saturated fat.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Honey Cake is a moist, fragrant baked dessert made from wheat flour, eggs, butter, sugar, and honey, where honey serves as both a primary sweetener and a flavour-defining ingredient. The cake is typically prepared by creaming butter and sugar, incorporating eggs, folding in sifted flour with leavening agents, and blending in honey before baking at moderate oven temperatures. The result is a soft, tender crumb with a golden-brown crust, a subtly floral sweetness, and a slightly denser texture than a plain sponge cake due to the hygroscopic nature of honey. In Singapore, honey cake is a popular café dessert offered by chains such as Toast Box, where it is served as a single slice alongside kopi (local coffee) or tea. Nutritionally, a standard 80 g slice provides approximately 288 kcal, 45.6 g carbohydrate, 25.6 g sugar, 9.6 g fat, and 4.4 g protein. The medium glycemic index (~56) and high glycemic load (~25.5 per serving) make it a food of concern for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes or insulin resistance. The saturated fat content from butter and eggs warrants moderation for those with cardiovascular disease. Honey cake is vegetarian and halal-suitable but contains gluten, dairy, and eggs, making it unsuitable for those with coeliac disease, dairy intolerance, or egg allergies.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a slice of honey cake?
Is honey cake suitable for diabetics?
What is the GI of honey cake?
Is Toast Box honey cake halal?
How much sugar is in honey cake?
What allergens are in honey cake?

confidence_reason: No exact match for Toast Box Honey Cake found in HPB Singapore Foodo, FAO/INFOODS SEA, or USDA FoodData Central branded entries. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy plain sponge cake (NDB 18101) with honey-enrichment adjustment. GI is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database sponge cake analogue. Confidence is medium due to the adapted rather than exact source match.
notes: Vitamin D (vitamin_d_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy sponge cake reference entry and is not reliably estimated for this product. Vitamin E (vitamin_e_mg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the primary source reference. Selenium (selenium_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the primary source reference. Trans fat (trans_fat_g) is set to 0.1 reflecting trace levels typical of butter-based baked goods; this is a plausible estimate consistent with USDA data for similar products. Vitamin C (vitamin_c_mg) is 0.0; confirmed zero as honey cake contains no vitamin-C-contributing ingredients and USDA SR Legacy reports 0 mg for plain sponge cake. GI of 56 is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database sponge cake data; no direct measurement for this specific product exists. Serving size of 80 g is estimated based on typical single-slice portions for café-style cakes in Singapore; the input record does not specify a gram weight. Nutrient values should be verified against Toast Box's official nutritional disclosure if and when published. HPB Foodo was checked and no matching honey cake entry was found at time of processing.
